be fair. In the fifth division animals that live on grass, ministers,
inmates of harems, and Vaisyas (or merchants) would suffer.
An eclipse occurring in the sixth division of the sky would harm
women and the Sudras, and lastly one occurring at sunset or the
last portion of the sky would destroy robbers and those that live
in the border countries. In whichever part of the sky the eclipse
ends, the people and things corresponding to that part would be
benefited.

Also —
In the case of a solar eclipse the daytime should be divided into
7 equal parts. One should then note down in which divisions
the eclipse begins, becomes maximum and ends. The persons
assigned to these will in order suffer,> be destroyed and* prosper.
Same is the case with a lunar eclipse, where the night i* to be
divided. From the text it is clear that certain castes, avccations
